title: Voroos
content:

'''Voroos''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]], [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of stationary predator that were found throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Due to a lack of [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], they could not chase prey, instead relying on camouflage and the use of their strong [[Tongue/Legends|tongues]] to capture prey and pull it into their large [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]]. [[Zoologist/Legends|Zoologists]] believed that the [[palak]] species of beetles were involved in voroos' [[Reproduction/Legends|reproduction]], but the subject was poorly understood.

==Biology and appearance==
A [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]], six-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]], predatory [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Reptile/Legends|reptile]], voroos measured between one and ten [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in diameter. The creatures camouflaged themselves to appear like natural geographical features, such as [[Moss/Legends|mossy]] hills and half-submerged [[Tree/Legends|tree]] trunks in [[Swamp/Legends|swamps]], or bare rocks and sand dunes in [[Desert/Legends|deserts]]. Lacking [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], voroos had [[Mouth/Legends|maws]] that were relatively large in comparison to the rest of their body, and were full of [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] and a strong, dexterous [[Tongue/Legends|tongue]]. Though not particularly dangerous, voroos' teeth made it difficult to remove anything from their mouths without damaging it.<ref name="CotG" />

==Behavior==
Voroos preferred [[Water/Legends|moist]] environments like swamps but were able to survive in almost any habitat, including areas as extreme as deserts and tundras. They depended on their camouflage to catch prey, as they were unable to chase them due to their lack of legs. When a meal approached, voroos would wrap their tongues around the prey to pull it into their gaping mouths. [[Zoologist/Legends|Zoologists]] were unsure of exactly how voroos [[Reproduction/Legends|reproduced]], although some theorized that near-microscopic beetles named [[palak]]s shared a large amount of their genome with voroos and, after swapping [[Gene/Legends|genetic]] material with them, carried the predators' fertilized gametes to new environments.<ref name="CotG" />

==Voroos in the galaxy==
Voroos could be found on many [[Planet/Legends|planets]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Zoologists did not fully understand the species' method of reproduction, but a theory involving the palak species of beetle sharing genetic material with voroos was the subject of some study.<ref name="CotG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Voroos were mentioned in ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', a [[1994]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The concept and illustration of voroos were provided by [[Tom O'Neill]], while the creature was designed by [[Chuck Truett]].<ref name="CotG" />
